summary: DHH celebrates Instiki, his wiki software, reaching the top spot on Rubyforge with over seven thousand downloads. He notes the even split between OS X, Linux/FreeBSD, and Windows users as evidence of broad cross-platform adoption. Despite not having time to actively develop Instiki, he's pleased it continues to grow and serve as a gateway to Ruby adoption. He acknowledges that Basecamp and Ruby on Rails take priority over Instiki development.
key_points: Instiki became the most downloaded application on Rubyforge with 7,000+ downloads
Downloads were evenly split across OS X, Linux/FreeBSD, and Windows, showing broad cross-platform appeal
Instiki served as a 'trojan horse' to increase Ruby language adoption
DHH's priorities had shifted to Basecamp and Ruby on Rails
money_quotes: The interesting thing is that the most recent version has an almost evenly divided split between .app, .tgz, and .zip downloads, which roughly translates to equal adoption by OS X'ers, Linux/FreeBSD'ers, and Windows people.
I've talked to many people who've caught interest in Ruby after using Instiki.
So as a trojan horse to increase Ruby adoption, it has been doing nicely as well.
key_insight: A well-crafted open source application can serve as a trojan horse for programming language adoption, drawing users to the underlying platform through the utility of the tool itself.
